purpose: Persona generation matrix — 5 personas x 3 prompts on 0.6B-Base + smoke adapter

qualitative_finding.evidence: All 15 generations produce degenerate output: leading Thai/Lao characters (กล้า, ไม่, มนุ) followed by long repeated runs of single characters (! or ㅋ). Korean output is essentially absent.
root_cause_hypothesis: 0.6B-Base + 200-iter LoRA at lr=2e-4 is severely undertrained for this domain.
[PERSONA]...[/PERSONA] tag conditioning was never reinforced in the smoke training data — the smoke adapter saw the comprehensive synth_lw.jsonl which uses different formatting.
Default sampling temp=0.8/top_p=0.9 amplifies tail collapse in an undertrained Korean model — multilingual base routes to higher-prior non-Korean character sets.
smoke adapter was trained for token-fire validation, NOT persona conditioning.
qualitative_finding.production_implication: This is NOT a regression in the production pipeline. Production persona-30 evaluation runs on RunPod with: (a) Qwen3-8B not 0.6B, (b) full Phase-3 SFT (epochs not iters) + Phase-4 ORPO not LoRA-only, (c) persona prompt format that matches sft_pairs.v3.jsonl conditioning.

distinguishability_metrics.honest_interpretation: The DISTINCT label is misleading: low overlap is because each persona collapsed to a DIFFERENT non-Korean character set, not because of true persona differentiation. This metric is artifact-of-failure, not signal-of-success. Re-run on RunPod 8B.

verdict_notes: Generation pipeline executes end-to-end (mlx_lm.generate + adapter loading + persona-tagged prompt template).
Quality is non-Korean gibberish — expected for 0.6B + 200-iter smoke adapter.
Persona-distinguishability evaluation is INCONCLUSIVE locally; defer to RunPod 8B + full SFT/ORPO.
Falls within Phase 5.7 directive 'fallback to 3 personas x 2 prompts on 0.6B adapter from C2' was not needed — full 5x3 matrix completed.
